scenarios.name: Coffee Shop
scenarios.description: Order coffee and cafe drinks easily
scenarios.tip: Self-order kiosks and pickup counters are common; short phrases matter most when changing ice, sugar, or takeaway.

scenarios.name: Street Food / Restaurant
scenarios.tip: Ticket machines, tablet menus, and set meals are common; short requests for water, takeaway, or spice level matter more than long explanations.

scenarios.name: Ride App / Taxi
scenarios.tip: Showing the destination screen is standard; short phrases for take me here, stop here, and payment method are more useful than long directions.

scenarios.name: Asking the Price
scenarios.tip: Bargaining is uncommon in Japan; use this scenario more for price confirmation, item choice, and receipt or tax-free follow-through.

scenarios.name: Polite Basics
scenarios.tip: Short polite forms matter more than casual friendliness; excuse me and thank you do a lot of work in travel moments.

scenarios.name: Convenience Store
scenarios.tip: Convenience stores solve many traveler problems fast: water, ATM, bag, receipt, charger, and quick checkout questions.

scenarios.name: Hotel / Hostel
scenarios.tip: Passport presentation, reservation lookup, checkout time, and luggage storage are common front-desk moments.

scenarios.name: Directions
scenarios.tip: Station names, exits, platforms, and whether something is walkable matter more than street names.

scenarios.name: Simple Problems
scenarios.tip: Show your screen early; repair phrases for slower speech, repetition, and lost-item help matter more than long explanations.

scenarios.name: Small Talk
scenarios.tip: Keep this light and optional; service usefulness matters more than forcing conversation in the first pack.

notes: Shared traveler baseline for Japanese, now reprioritized around polite utility, show-screen readability, and non-Latin search caution. Fill translation, pronunciation, and audio posture before runtime wiring.
